flashcards.back: A class-level member that is shared among all objects of the class and exists independently of any object instances.

flashcards.front: How do you access static members?
flashcards.back: Using the class name and scope resolution operator (ClassName::staticMember) or through object instances (object.staticMember).

flashcards.front: Can static functions access non-static members?
flashcards.back: No, static functions cannot directly access non-static members because they don't have access to the 'this' pointer.

flashcards.front: Where must static data members be defined?
flashcards.back: Outside the class declaration using the scope resolution operator, typically in the implementation file.

flashcards.front: What is the lifetime of static members?
flashcards.back: Static members exist for the entire duration of the program execution, from program start to program termination.
